sourceEvidence.label: Proximal Humerus Fracture — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Proximal Humerus Fracture

objectives: Recognize common proximal humerus fracture patterns and demographic.
Understand why many are treated nonoperatively.

sourceEvidence.label: Proximal Humerus Fracture — Topic metadata
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Topic metadata for Proximal Humerus Fracture

sourceEvidence.label: Proximal Humerus Fracture — Fast study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Fast study template for Proximal Humerus Fracture

mustKnow: Often low-energy elderly injury, many nonoperative, check axillary nerve
payload.oneLiner: Most proximal humerus fractures are elderly fragility injuries where acceptable alignment often beats risky surgery.
pimpQuestions: Why are many treated without surgery?
orSurvivalTips: Mention tuberosity displacement and head split if present
Do not forget elbow motion during rehab

sourceEvidence.label: Proximal Humerus Fracture — Deep study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Deep study template for Proximal Humerus Fracture

classification: Displacement-based part concept at a high level
imaging: AP, scapular Y, axillary views if tolerated
decisionMaking: Displacement, tuberosity involvement, age, bone quality, function
boardPearls: Axillary nerve exam should be part of every shoulder trauma presentation

57 Orthobullets question mapping(s) for trauma-proximal-humerus-fractures (metadata only — no stems/answers)

metadata.description: Fracture involving the proximal humeral head, neck, or tuberosities.
Post-fracture shoulder stiffness from immobilization or malunion.
Part-based proximal humerus fracture classification framework.
Avascular necrosis of the humeral head after proximal humerus fracture.

metadata.claim_text: Axillary nerve exam should be part of every proximal humerus fracture presentation.
Many proximal humerus fractures in elderly patients are treated nonoperatively when alignment is acceptable.
Pushing ORIF in poor bone quality complex fractures without discussing hemiarthroplasty alternatives.

metadata.action: Discuss ORIF with attending; document tuberosity reduction goals and rehab plan
metadata.trigger: Displaced two-, three-, or four-part pattern with reconstructable tuberosities in suitable patient

metadata.claim_text: Tuberosity displacement and head-split patterns change operative thinking more than nondisplaced neck lines alone.
Stiffness is a major functional complication after proximal humerus fracture and immobilization.
State fracture parts, axillary nerve function, sling plan, and early pendulum motion when safe.

metadata.action: Discuss hemiarthroplasty with attending and tuberosity healing expectations
metadata.trigger: Unreconstructable head-split or severe comminution in elderly low-demand patient

metadata.description: Open reduction internal fixation of displaced proximal humerus fracture.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Displaced Proximal Humerus Fracture

metadata.description: Multi-part or displaced proximal humerus pattern altering fixation versus arthroplasty calculus.
Hemiarthroplasty for select complex or unreconstructable proximal humerus fractures.
